item.title: 1908-10-07 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1908-10-07 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1908-10-07 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
One in a series of recipe advertisements put out by George Dauth to advertise his 9th Street Market.

Transcription
Spanish Stew
"Cut beef into small pieces, then boil until tender and brown. Turn into this two onions an half can of tomatoes that have been fried in little butter or lard, mix with little flour and water to make gravy. Boil up and serve.; To obtain best results with these Recipes the materials should be of the best quality. Dauth's Ninth St. Market deals in that kind only."

People
George Dauth

Details
Date: 1908-10-07
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Source: "Spanish Stew." The Greeley Tribune [USA, Colorado, Greeley], 1908-10-07, p. 8
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/advertisement/1908-10-07-the-greeley-tribune-george-dauth-store-recipe-advertisement/

The post 1908-10-07 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1908-09-30 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1908-09-30 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1908-09-30 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
One in a series of recipe advertisements put out by George Dauth to advertise his 9th Street Market.

Transcription
German Veal Pot Roast
"Five pounds lean veal, brown one-half cup butter in a kettle, put in meat, brown well on all sides, being careful not to burn it, then add a large cup of thick sour cream; turn meat until the cream is thoroughly browned, add salt, one bay leaf, pepper and water enough to boil about two hours. Let it almost boil down, remove meat, add water and flour to thicken for gravy and strain. Very nice.; To obtain best results with these Recipes the materials should be of the best quality. Dauth's Ninth St. Market deals in that kind only."

People
George Dauth

Details
Date: 1908-09-30
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Source: "German Veal Pot Roast." The Greeley Tribune [USA, Colorado, Greeley], 1908-09-30, p. 8
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/advertisement/1908-09-30-the-greeley-tribune-george-dauth-store-recipe-advertisement/

The post 1908-09-30 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1908-09-23 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1908-09-23 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1908-09-23 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
One in a series of recipe advertisements put out by George Dauth to advertise his 9th Street Market.

Transcription
Chicken Croquettes
"To one three-pound chicken add one pair of sweetbreads, chop the meat and measure to one point of meat take one-half pint of milk, one large spoon butter, two tablespoons flour, one teaspoon chopped parsley, one teaspoon onion juice, one teaspoon salt, one-fourth teaspoon pepper, one-fourth nutmeg, a pinch of cayenne. Mix well, make into 14 croquettes, dip in mixture of one egg and one tablespoon of hot water. Roll in cracker crumbs and fry in hot fat.; To obtain best results with these Recipes the materials should be of the best quality. Dauth's Ninth St. Market deals in that kind only."

People
George Dauth

Details
Date: 1908-09-23
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Source: "Chicken Croquettes." The Greeley Tribune [USA, Colorado, Greeley], 1908-09-23, p. 14
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/advertisement/1908-09-23-the-greeley-tribune-george-dauth-store-recipe-advertisement/

The post 1908-09-23 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1908-09-16 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1908-09-16 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1908-09-16 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
One in a series of recipe advertisements put out by George Dauth to advertise his 9th Street Market.

Transcription
Baked Sweetbreads
"Three sweetbreads, one egg and bread crumbs; put the sweetbreads into warm water for an hour to draw out the blood, then put them into boiling water and simmer for ten minutes which makes them firm. Drain them, brush over with the beaten egg, roll in bread-crumbs and dip in egg again, sprinkle with fresh bread-crumbs the second time, pour a little melted butter over them and put into a moderate oven to bake for three-quarters of an hour. Place the sweetbreads on toast and pour around them, but not over, a good brown gravy. Or they may be prepared in the same manner and fried instead of baked.; To obtain best results with these Recipes the materials should be of the best quality. Dauth's Ninth St. Market deals in that kind only."

People
George Dauth

Details
Date: 1908-09-16
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Source: "Baked Sweetbreads." The Greeley Tribune [USA, Colorado, Greeley], 1908-09-16, p. 4
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/advertisement/1908-09-16-the-greeley-tribune-george-dauth-store-recipe-advertisement/

The post 1908-09-16 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1908-09-02 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1908-09-02 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1908-09-02 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
One in a series of recipe advertisements put out by George Dauth to advertise his 9th Street Market.

Transcription
Stuffed Roast Pork
"A small loin of pork, three table-spoons bread crumbs, one onion, half tea-spoon chopped sage, half teaspoon salt, half teaspoon pepper, one ounce chopped suet, one tablespoon drippings. Separate each joint of the loin with the meat chopper, making an incision with a knife into the thick part of the pork, in which to put the stuffing. Prepare the stuffing by mixing the bread crumbs together with the onion, which must be chopped fine; add to this the sage, pepper salt and suet, mix all well together and press snugly into the incision already made in the pork and sew together the edges of the meat to confine the stuffing. Grease well a sheet of kitchen paper with drippings, place the loin into this, securing it with wrapping of twine. Bake in dry baking pan in brisk oven basting immediately and constantly as grease draws out. Bake, allowing 20 minutes to the pound and 20 minutes longer. Serve with apple sauce or fritters.; To obtain best results with these Recipes the materials should be of the best quality. Dauth's Ninth St. Market deals in that kind only."

People
George Dauth

Details
Date: 1908-09-02
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Source: "Stuffed Roast Pork." The Greeley Tribune [USA, Colorado, Greeley], 1908-09-02, p. 4
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/advertisement/1908-09-02-the-greeley-tribune-george-dauth-store-recipe-advertisement/

The post 1908-09-02 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1908-08-19 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1908-08-19 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1908-08-19 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
One in a series of recipe advertisements put out by George Dauth to advertise his 9th Street Market.

Transcription
Spiced Liver
"Split a calf’s liver, parboil, scoop out center, mince this, add one onion minced, chopped parsley, sage, salt, pepper, stale bread crumbs and 2 tablespoonsful butter; fill cavities, fasten edges with skewers, put in pan; baste with water, vinegar and spices while cooking. Garnish with fried noodles.; To obtain best results with these Recipes the materials should be of the best quality. Dauth's Ninth St. Market deals in that kind only."

People
George Dauth

Details
Date: 1908-08-19
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Source: "Spiced Liver." The Greeley Tribune [USA, Colorado, Greeley], 1908-08-19, p. 7
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/advertisement/1908-08-19-the-greeley-tribune-george-dauth-store-recipe-advertisement/

The post 1908-08-19 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1908-08-12 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1908-08-12 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1908-08-12 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
One in a series of recipe advertisements put out by George Dauth to advertise his 9th Street Market.

Transcription
Beef A La Mode
"Take three pounds of fresh beef, remove all gristle and fat, put in pan and pour over a scant cup of vinegar in which spices, cloves, mace, allspice and black pepper have been mixed; add a pint of soup stock. Cover and cook slowly until tender and brown.; To obtain best results with these Recipes the materials should be of the best quality. Dauth's Ninth St. Market deals in that kind only."

People
George Dauth

Details
Date: 1908-08-12
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Source: "Beef A La Mode." The Greeley Tribune [USA, Colorado, Greeley], 1908-08-12, p. 7
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/advertisement/1908-08-12-the-greeley-tribune-george-dauth-store-recipe-advertisement/

The post 1908-08-12 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1908-08-05 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1908-08-05 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1908-08-05 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
One in a series of recipe advertisements put out by George Dauth to advertise his 9th Street Market.

Transcription
Lamb or Mutton Cutlets
"Trim cutlets, removing superfluous fat; dip in melted butter and roll in grated cheese and bread crumbs of equal parts. Cook over hot fire being careful not to burn. Serve with brown sauce. ; To obtain best results with these Recipes the materials should be of the best quality. Dauth's Ninth St. Market deals in that kind only."

People
George Dauth

Details
Date: 1908-08-05
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Source: "Lamb or Mutton Cutlets." The Greeley Tribune [USA, Colorado, Greeley], 1908-08-05, p. 12
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/advertisement/1908-08-05-the-greeley-tribune-george-dauth-store-recipe-advertisement/

The post 1908-08-05 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1908-07-29 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1908-07-29 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1908-07-29 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
One in a series of recipe advertisements put out by George Dauth to advertise his 9th Street Market.

Transcription
Kidney with Chili
"Wash and dry the kidney and cut up fine, also one medium sized onion, have lard right hot in frying pan, put in kidney and onion and let it fry; add pepper and salt, 1 bay leaf, 2 tablespoonsful of chili powder and a little flour; lastly, enough hot water for sauce, and vinegar to taste.; To obtain best results with these Recipes the materials should be of the best quality. Dauth's Ninth St. Market deals in that kind only."

People
George Dauth

Details
Date: 1908-07-29
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Source: "Kidney with Chili." The Greeley Tribune [USA, Colorado, Greeley], 1908-07-29, p. 8
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/advertisement/1908-07-29-the-greeley-tribune-george-dauth-store-recipe-advertisement/

The post 1908-07-29 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.

item.title: 1908-07-22 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post 1908-07-22 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
item.content:encoded:
1908-07-22 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement
One in a series of recipe advertisements put out by George Dauth to advertise his 9th Street Market.

Transcription
Stuffed Beef’s Heart
"Wash the heart and remove all muscular portion inside. Wash with vinegar and rub with red pepper and spice, set aside over night. Prepare an ordinary bread dressing and sew up the openings. Slowly cover with boiling water in which is an onion cut fine and bay leaf. Stew slowly for two hours. Then transfer to buttered earthen dish and bake two hours. ; To obtain best results with these Recipes the materials should be of the best quality. Dauth's Ninth St. Market deals in that kind only."

People
George Dauth

Details
Date: 1908-07-22
Location: USA, Colorado, Greeley
Source: "Stuffed Beef’s Heart." The Greeley Tribune [USA, Colorado, Greeley], 1908-07-22, p. 12
Permalink: https://dauthfamily.github.io/advertisement/1908-07-22-the-greeley-tribune-george-dauth-store-recipe-advertisement/

The post 1908-07-22 – The Greeley Tribune – George Dauth Store Recipe Advertisement appeared first on Dauth Family Archive.
